title: Transactions and ACID Properties
summary: Understand database transactions and the ACID properties that guarantee data integrity.
content_html:
A transaction is a sequence of database operations treated as a single unit. Either all operations succeed, or none are applied.

ACID Properties

  • A — Atomicity: All operations in a transaction complete, or none do (all-or-nothing)
  • C — Consistency: Transaction takes the database from one valid state to another, maintaining all rules
  • I — Isolation: Concurrent transactions execute as if they were sequential (no interference)
  • D — Durability: Once committed, changes persist even after system failure (written to disk)

Transaction Commands

BEGIN TRANSACTION;
  UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE id=1;
  UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE id=2;
COMMIT;   -- saves changes

-- OR if error:
ROLLBACK; -- undoes all changes

Concurrency Problems (Without Isolation)

  • Dirty read: reading uncommitted data from another transaction
  • Non-repeatable read: same query returns different results within one transaction
  • Phantom read: new rows appear in repeated queries within same transaction

Isolation Levels

Read Uncommitted → Read Committed → Repeatable Read → Serializable (increasing strictness)

exercises.title: ACID A
options: All operations in a transaction complete, or none do
Data is consistent before and after
exercises.answer: All operations in a transaction complete, or none do

exercises.title: ACID C
options: The transaction brings the database from one valid state to another
exercises.answer: The transaction brings the database from one valid state to another

exercises.title: ACID I
options: Concurrent transactions execute as if sequential (no interference)
exercises.answer: Concurrent transactions execute as if sequential (no interference)

exercises.title: ACID D
options: Committed changes persist even after system failure
exercises.answer: Committed changes persist even after system failure

exercises.title: ROLLBACK
options: Undoes all changes made since BEGIN TRANSACTION
exercises.answer: Undoes all changes made since BEGIN TRANSACTION

exercises.title: Dirty read
options: Transaction reads uncommitted data from another transaction
exercises.answer: Transaction reads uncommitted data from another transaction

exercises.title: Bank example
exercises.prompt: In a bank transfer (debit account A, credit account B), atomicity ensures:
options: Both debit AND credit happen, or neither does
exercises.answer: Both debit AND credit happen, or neither does

exercises.title: Strictest isolation
exercises.prompt: The strictest isolation level that prevents all concurrency anomalies is:

exercises.title: Phantom read
options: New rows appear in a repeated query within the same transaction
A row's value changes between two reads
exercises.answer: New rows appear in a repeated query within the same transaction
